questionHtml:
What is the enthalpy change of the reaction?

C6H14 (l) → C2H4 (g) + C4H10 (g)

A.  + 1411 + 2878 + 4163

B.  + 1411 − 2878 − 4163

C.  + 1411 + 2878 − 4163

D.  − 1411 − 2878 + 4163

meta.title: What is the enthalpy change of the reaction? C6H14 (l) → C2H4 (g) + C4H10 (g) A. + 1411 + 2878 + 4163 B. + 1411 − 2878 − 4163 C. + 1411 + 2878 − 4163 D. − 1411 − 2878 + 4163
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 1.2.2—Hess’s law states that the enthalpy change for a reaction is independent of the pathway between the initial and final states. Apply Hess’s law to calculate enthalpy changes in multistep reactions.
